% Auto-generated by experiments/build_qualitative_example.py
\begin{figure}[!htb]
\centering
\fbox{\begin{minipage}{0.96\linewidth}
\small
\textbf{Qualitative example of the refinement paradox} \\
\textit{Dataset:} squad \quad \textit{Generator:} mistral \\

\textbf{Question.}~What city did Super Bowl 50 take place in? \\
\textbf{Ground truth.}~\textit{Santa Clara}

\medskip
\textbf{Baseline (no refinement).} ~ faithfulness $=0.942$,
\emph{not hallucinated}. \\
\textsc{Answer:}~The Super Bowl 50 took place in Santa Clara, which is part of the San Francisco Bay Area.

\medskip
\textbf{HCPC-v1 (refine all).} ~ faithfulness $=0.478$,
\emph{hallucinated}. \\
\textsc{Answer:}~San Francisco Bay Area

\medskip
\textbf{HCPC-v2 (selective).} ~ faithfulness $=0.942$,
\emph{not hallucinated} (recovered). \\
\textsc{Answer:}~The Super Bowl 50 took place in Santa Clara, which is part of the San Francisco Bay Area.

\medskip
\textbf{Reading.} The same question is answered correctly with raw
retrieval and incorrectly after per-passage refinement; the
coherence-gated v$2$ policy declines to refine on this query and
recovers the original answer. The faithfulness gap baseline\,$\to$\,v$1$
is $+0.464$.
\end{minipage}}
\caption{Qualitative example of the refinement paradox on squad
with mistral. Per-passage refinement raises retrieval similarity but
strips the connective evidence the generator was relying on; the
generator silently fills the gap with a plausible but unsupported
claim.}
\label{fig:qualitative_paradox}
\end{figure}
